data.title: bd-1lpe.1: Define MergeState enum and persisted merge-state file format
data.description: Implements merge state machine and atomic file persistence. MergePhase enum (7 variants, forward-only transitions), MergeStateFile struct, ValidationResult, MergeStateError. Atomic writes via temp+fsync+rename. 30 unit tests.

data.reason: Security review of merge_state.rs (implementation landed in crates/maw-core/src/merge_state.rs; seal diff unavailable — review is jj-anchored and the change is not present in this git repo, so inline anchoring failed). No CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM issues. Pure state-machine + atomic file I/O; no command exec, no network/injection surface, no unsafe, forward-only transitions enforced, serde_json parse is bounded. Two LOW defense-in-depth notes: (1) write_atomic() uses fs::File::create on a fixed-name temp '.merge-state.tmp' which follows symlinks and truncates the target — a create_new(true) O_EXCL temp with a randomized/pid suffix would harden it and also be collision-safe under concurrent writers (though the epoch lock serializes today); low severity in maw's local single-owner trust model. (2) ValidationResult/CommandResult persist raw command+stdout+stderr verbatim into .manifold/merge-state.json (and recovery snapshots) in plaintext — validation output can capture secrets/tokens on failure; consider redaction or documenting the secrets-at-rest posture. Neither blocks. LGTM.
